arts.ttl: Financial police seize €75 million from Elkann brothers and Juventus president Ferrero
arts.sum: The Investigating Judge of the Turin Prosecutor’s Office has ordered the seizure, carried out by the Financial Police, of financial assets, movable and immovable property worth €74.8 million from brothers John, Lapo and Ginevra Elkann, as well as Juventus President Gianluca Ferrero (longtime accountant for the Agnelli/Elkann family) and Urs Robert Von Grunigen (notary of Marella Agnelli) for “tax fraud” and “fraud against the State.” Calcio e Finanza report:“As part of the criminal proceedings against Gianluca Ferrero, Urs Robert Von Grünigen and brothers John Philip, Lapo Edovard and Ginevra Elkann, the Preliminary Investigations Judge has issued – at the request The post Financial police seize €75 million from Elkann brothers and Juventus president Ferrero appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Napoli open door for Osimhen to Chelsea
arts.sum: Victor Osimhen does not want to play for Napoli again, sporting director Giovanni Manna admitted, opening the door to the Nigeria international departing for Chelsea or another Premier League destination in the future. In the end, a switch to Galatasaray was not what either Osimhen or Napoli planned, yet Manna admits it was a logical solution that threatened to end in an ugly statement.

arts.ttl: Juventus-Napoli: Official ban on Napoli fans, already sold tickets cancelled
arts.sum: Authorities have chosen to prohibit away travel and ticket sales to all residents of the Campania region for the Juventus-Napoli match, also ordering the cancellation of tickets already sold so far. In recent days, law enforcement had raised concerns about managing the arrival of Napoli fans in the city. Calciomercato report that about 2,300 tickets had already been sold in the away sector, but over 3,000 ultras from Naples were expected in the city. The danger of clashes, especially after what happened during the last away match against Cagliari, was present and high. For this reason, the Prefecture and Police The post Juventus-Napoli: Official ban on Napoli fans, already sold tickets cancelled appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Juventus v Napoli: Match Preview
arts.sum: Two of Italian football’s heavyweights go head-to-head on Saturday evening as Juventus welcome Napoli to the Allianz Stadium in a crucial early-season Serie A encounter. Both sides harbor title ambitions this campaign and will be eager to lay down a marker with victory in this high-profile clash. The Bianconeri have made a solid if unspectacular start to the new season under new manager Thiago Motta. They remain unbeaten after four league games, though back-to-back 0-0 draws against Roma and Empoli have seen them slip to fourth in the table. arts.ttl: Thiago Motta: ‘I expect a great game against Napoli’
arts.sum: Juventus manager Thiago Motta expressed confidence ahead of his team’s upcoming Serie A clash against Napoli. Speaking at a pre-match press conference, Motta emphasized the importance of the fixture and shared insights on his squad’s preparation. “I expect a great match, a confrontation with strong players on the field,” Motta stated. “We will approach the game with great seriousness and responsibility, always respecting the opponent but playing our football.” The manager confirmed that defender Federico Gatti would be available for selection, with only Conceicao and Milik ruled out due to injury.
